evals.prompt: Do a full codebase review of this project.
evals.expected_output: A prioritized, plain-language report that catches the deliberate issues planted in the fixture app: a hardcoded service-role-shaped key, a table with no RLS, a client-only admin check, select('*'), and a silent catch.
expectations: Flags the hardcoded key-shaped string in src/lib/config.ts as a critical/high-severity finding
Flags that the quotes table has no RLS enabled
Flags the isAdmin-only client-side check as insufficient access control
Flags the .select('*') call on the quotes table
Uses the report structure with a Scorecard and Critical/Important/Improvements sections, not a raw list
Explains findings in plain business-impact language, not just rule names

evals.prompt: Review this codebase, but I only care about the security section right now: security
evals.expected_output: Runs only the security section, scoped by the $ARGUMENTS convention, and skips performance/best-practices output.
expectations: Restricts the review to the security dimension only, per the $ARGUMENTS convention described in the skill
Does not produce a performance or best-practices section
Still flags the hardcoded key and missing RLS from the fixture
